docs.title: Decompiler Problem Inventory
docs.text:
• Date: 2026-07-10
  • Scope: Shipped NIR/HIR decompile quality (x86/x86-64 primary)
  • Related: ADR 0008, ADR 0009, ISA debt table

docs.title: Executive summary
docs.text: Current quality gaps are dominated by intra-function NIR owner bugs (materialize, terminator, structuring, normalize), not by missing static FactStore depth or SMT solver coupling. Static/type facts and solver/emulator help later ceilings (API types, path validation); they do not fix cmov/return/ binding-order failures by themselves.

docs.title: Priority ranking (ROI)
docs.text: Pri Theme Owner Why high ROI now P0 Distinct live values share one binding (param/sum/return collapse) materialize / variable_merge Wrong C that cannot compile or is semantically false P0 Stack-param → reg alias does not dominate first use materialize (hoist is bandage) Use-before-def; D1 emergency pass P0 Return value recovery on epilogue joins / cmov arms terminator Empty or wrong return P1 Flag/cmov chains not folded to relational values materialize + flag recovery classify_range / clamp noise P1 Control structure (goto, nested if, switch-like) structuring saturating_add / classify_range readability P2 Type/role (size_t vs pointer, signatures) type recovery + static facts checksum-style rows P3 Broader static FactStore fixed-point fission-static + decompiler Real-world API ceilings P3 Solver in decomp hot path — Prefer emulator/validation lane

docs.title: What is not the main problem (for now)
docs.text:
• “We don’t depend on static enough” — partial only; static helps types/API, not local binding/control facts.
  • “We need SMT in the decompiler core” — wrong layer for most residual rows; keep fission-solver with emulator / optional narrow queries.

docs.title: Concrete residual signals (m32 O2 control_flow)
docs.text:
Measured with release CLI against control_flow_gcc-m32_O2.exe (2026-07-10).

Function Status Top defects count_bits Good Loop + return OK clamp Partial Semantic shape OK; flag temps (of/sf/zf) noise; hoist-dependent param order signum Partial Returns 1 / -setnz path; extra block braces; return type uchar checksum Partial Loop OK; len typed as uchar *; cast noise saturating_add Good m32/gcc O0+O2 5/5. Tail-cmov INT_MIN + primary-return HW name; printer shows -2147483648. Nesting/flag noise residual. classify_range Improving m32 O2 5/5 after xor+setcc partial-reg composition (try_lower_zero_extended_partial_register recognizes xor reg,reg and zero-before-setcc). High path folds to (value != 10) + 2. Low path still flag-noisy (setnz/neg/cmovg). gcc O2 residual 4/5.

docs.title: F1 — Binding / value identity
docs.text:
Invariant: Distinct reaching definitions (params, sum, INT_MIN, INT_MAX) must not be force-merged into one name when path-sensitive or when storage is reused only by the ABI return register after a real intermediate value exists.

Evidence: saturating_add overwrites eax with both params then uses eax + eax.

Forbidden: Row-specific renames; “always keep eax” without def-use.

docs.title: F2 — Dominating param aliases
docs.text:
Invariant: After recovering stack_slot → param_N into a register, the assignment reg = param_N dominates first use of reg in the emitted HIR.

Evidence: clamp without hoist_param_alias → use-before-def on edx.

docs.title: F3 — Primary return at joins
docs.text:
Invariant: When RET’s p-code input is a return address, the returned expression is the live primary return register (or equal pred exprs when safe).

Evidence: epilogue multi-pred join (partially fixed 2026-07-10).

docs.title: F4 — Relational recovery from flags/cmov
docs.text:
Invariant: Cmp+flag+cmov/setcc sequences lower to relational ops or select forms without mandatory dead flag stores in final HIR.

Evidence: classify_range, clamp LE arm still dumps of/sf/zf.

docs.title: F5 — Structuring legality vs readability
docs.text: Invariant: Acyclic diamonds and saturating branches become if/else without spurious goto when CFG postdom facts allow; switch-like chains prefer value ranges when legal.

docs.title: F6 — Type/role
docs.text: Invariant: Scalar accumulators and length parameters are not stuck as pointer types without address-producing uses.

docs.title: Improvement plan (this cycle)
docs.text:
1. P0 F1: Fix saturating_add-class binding collapse (distinct live values on return-reg timeline) — synthetic test + real decomp.
  2. P0 F2: Materialize entry stack-param aliases in load order → retire hoist when real clamp clean.
  3. P1 F4/F5: classify_range / residual saturating control structure.
  4. Track debt D1/D3+ in ISA inventory; no new emergency passes without removal path.

docs.title: Non-goals this cycle
docs.text:
• Big-bang static FactStore rewrite
  • SMT in normalize hot path
  • New ISA breadth beyond x86/x86-64 measurement

docs.title: ISA Semantic Debt Inventory (P0)
docs.text:
• Date: 2026-07-10
  • Scope: NIR semantic path under crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/ (builder/materialize, normalize, structuring)
  • Policy: ADR 0009
  • Method: Manual grep + owner review of CallingConvention::* gates, emergency normalize passes, env-gated materialize features, and shared helpers introduced by recent m32/cmov/signum quality work.
  • Non-goal: Big-bang rewrite. This table is the refactor backlog for opportunistic cleanup when touching related owners.

docs.title: Status legend
docs.text: Status Meaning OK Shared CFG / def-use / register-family invariant; extend, do not fork MODEL Allowed ISA surface (cspec, namer, CC slot tables, stack-param classification) DEBT Semantic meaning gated on ISA string/CC, or emergency cleanup papering over an earlier owner ENV Opt-in env flag; treat as experimental debt until default-on with an invariant owner

docs.title: Shared helpers (OK — prefer extending)
docs.text:
Helper Owner path Invariant (ISA-agnostic statement) Loop-carried register update detection builder/materialize/loop_carried/ (shape.rs, binding.rs, seed.rs) Register-space def that reads itself (or is live across a backedge) keeps a stable binding name; param/HW names preferred over fresh temps Same-block forward branch resolve nir/cfg.rs → same_block_forward_branch_target_op_idx CBranch target that lands later in the same block is a skip edge (relative seq or absolute code address), not an always-taken guarded Copy Return-join live-in guard structuring/linear/mod.rs → forwarding_block_defines_return_tail_live_in A single-successor “forward” arm that defines a join Return’s live-in (including primary return reg) is not a trivial empty forward Unconditional-only copy merge normalize/recovery/variable_merge.rs → collect_direct_copies Only top-level unconditional v = w establishes merge identity; path-sensitive cmov/if copies must not collapse clamp/min/max arms Loop-carried candidate shape loop_carried/shape.rs → is_loop_carried_register_update_candidate Register-space, non-constant, size ≥ 4 — no CC enum on the shape check

These are the patterns ADR 0009 expects new quality work to extend.

docs.title: MODEL surfaces (allowed — keep differences here)
docs.text: Surface Owner path Notes Register namer / primary return / param slots nir/cspec/register_model.rs, cspec/apply.rs CC tables map offsets → names and return/param sets Stack-parameter slot classification builder/memory/stack_slots.rs, nir/abi.rs Offset tables per CC are model data Stack push / call-arg recovery (x86-32 push idiom) builder/calls/call_recovery.rs Encoding decode into stack-arg list; policy of “call has N args” must stay CC-table driven Status-flag offset map (x86 EFLAGS bits) register_model.rs hw_name_at 0x200.. Naming model; consumers should ask “is flag register?” via namer/pspec, not hard-code offsets

docs.title: DEBT rows (semantic cores / emergency)
docs.text: ID Location Kind Issue Preferred fix direction D1 normalize/cleanup/temp_var.rs → hoist_param_alias_copies_before_first_use Emergency pass Still required (2026-07-10): synthetic clamp OK without hoist; real m32 O2 clamp regresses (edx use-before-def). Root: materialize does not emit dominating stack-param→reg alias for value reg before first compare Keep hoist until materialize emits entry stack-param aliases in load order; see docs/proposals/2026-07-10-retire-hoist-param-alias.md (aborted) D2 builder/materialize/loop_carried/binding.rs → loop_header_external_seed_binding_name_for_update ISA admission gate ~~Early return None unless x86 CCs~~ Fixed 2026-07-10: admit via param slot / is_primary_return_register / register-space candidate only Was: drop CC gate. Done in docs/proposals/2026-07-10-loop-external-seed-cc-gate.md D3 builder/materialize/no_consumer.rs → is_x86_status_flag_output Hard-coded offsets Suppress no-consumer pure flag defs using raw 0x200.. offsets Query cspec/pspec “flag group” or namer is_flag_register; keep pure-RHS check D4 builder/expr/lower_expr.rs → stack_pointer_register_name CC-local name table X86_32 special-case ESP/EBP offsets before namer Prefer namer/cspec only; delete dual path once namer covers m32 frame regs D5 builder/calls/call_recovery.rs → is_x86_32_esp / push helpers Encoding helper with CC guard ESP detection hard-codes offset 0x10 + X86_32 Generalize to “stack pointer varnode” from cspec; keep push-store scan as encoding decode D6 normalize/recovery/flag_recovery.rs → is_x86_flag_variable Name-string ISA gate Flag recovery keyed on x86 flag name strings Prefer typed flag facts from materialize/cspec; name lists as fallback only D7 normalize/idioms/prologue.rs (and related) HW name lists Prologue recognition via raw rbx/rbp/… / ARM r4… lists Frame / callee-save sets from cspec; keep as MODEL if moved into tables D8 builder/control/terminator.rs (Arm32 branches) CC-forked control paths Multiple calling_convention == Arm32 branches in terminator Shared join/return/live-in helpers with CC-supplied register sets (same pattern as recent x86 return-join work)

docs.title: Priority for P0 remediation
docs.text:
1. D1 — emergency pass; highest architectural cost if left permanent
  2. D2 — one-line admission gate blocking non-x86 reuse of a good helper
  3. D3–D5 — model leakage into materialize/call recovery (batch when touching namer/cspec)
  4. D6–D8 — normalize/terminator cleanup when next quality cycle hits those owners

docs.title: ENV experimental flags (do not grow without owners)
docs.text:
Flag Path Role FISSION_ENABLE_NO_CONSUMER_SUPPRESSION materialize/no_consumer.rs Gates broader no-consumer suppress path FISSION_ENABLE_PARITY_CHAIN_MATERIALIZATION materialize/same_block.rs Experimental parity-chain materialize FISSION_ENABLE_STACK_ADDR_FRAME_STABLE_REPLACEMENT materialize/same_block.rs Frame-stable stack-addr replacement FISSION_ENABLE_COPY_OVERWRITE_RESTART materialize/cross_block.rs Cross-block restart experiment FISSION_ENABLE_PREDICATE_REFRESH_RESTART materialize/cross_block.rs Predicate refresh restart FISSION_ENABLE_EXPLICIT_MERGE_BINDING materialize/cross_block.rs Explicit merge binding FISSION_ENABLE_BLOCKGRAPH_COLLAPSE / FISSION_ENABLE_MIR_BLOCKGRAPH structuring/driver/admission.rs Alternate structuring admission FISSION_ENABLE_WIDE_DEAD_ASSIGNMENT_RERUN_ADMISSION normalize/analysis/defuse.rs Dead-assign rerun admission FISSION_COLLAPSE_LOOP structuring/collapse_loop.rs Loop collapse opt-in FISSION_STRUCTURING_ENGINE types/options.rs Engine selection

Debug-only (FISSION_PREVIEW_DEBUG, FISSION_PREVIEW_DIAG, FISSION_PREVIEW_PERF, …) are observability, not semantic debt.

Rule: New production defaults must not add FISSION_ENABLE_* knobs. Promote only after an invariant owner + tests; otherwise remove or leave off.

docs.title: What is not debt (recent quality work)
docs.text:
These were motivated by m32 rows but restated as common facts (ADR 0009 §3):

  • Absolute + relative same-block CBranch resolution for cmov chains
  • Loop-carried IntAdd/IntRight name stability
  • Conditional-copy merge barrier (cmov arms)
  • Return-arm live-in vs trivial-forward (signum-class diamonds)
  • Structuring has no CallingConvention:: matches under nir/structuring/ (good separation)

docs.title: Review checklist (when editing NIR semantics)
docs.text:
• [ ] Condition is CFG / dominance / def-use / register family / ABI slot, not == X86_32 alone
  • [ ] Encoding decode (push, cmov absolute target, EFLAGS offset) ends in a shared fact
  • [ ] No new end-of-pipeline normalize pass if materialize/structuring can own the binding
  • [ ] Extend an OK helper above when the failure family matches
  • [ ] Update this table when a DEBT row is fixed or a new emergency pass is added

docs.title: Related docs
docs.text:
• ADR 0009 — ISA-agnostic semantic rules
  • ADR 0008 — NIR substrate / owner boundaries
  • NIR AGENTS.md
  • Older token scan (noisy): 2026-07-04-arch-isolation-scan.md

docs.title: Fission v0.1.2
docs.text:
Fission 0.1.2 is a decompiler-quality, SLEIGH parity, and execution-foundation release. Compared with 0.1.1, it substantially expands semantic recovery in the NIR pipeline, tightens raw p-code agreement with Ghidra, introduces dual NIR/HIR rendering, and adds experimental native emulation and solver foundations.

This is a large patch release during Fission's pre-1.0 development period. The documented CLI remains the primary supported interface, but output produced by the decompiler can change as recovery and structuring improve. The new sandbox, symbolic execution, and native solver surfaces are experimental.

docs.title: Release summary
docs.text:
Area v0.1.1 v0.1.2 Decompiler feedback DecompContext and fact feedback foundation Broader variable, type, return, loop, and conditional recovery built on that foundation P-code parity Initial native SLEIGH pipeline Ghidra-aligned branch targets, inst_next, USEROP indexing, and conversion semantics Output layers Single primary pseudocode path Explicit NIR/HIR presentation selection with decomp --layer CFG structuring Iterative structuring feedback Additional diamond, natural-loop, epilogue-return, short-circuit, and tail-CMOV recovery Type recovery Initial fact propagation Storage-role separation, reaching definitions, partial-register handling, and scalar/address constraints Execution Debugger-oriented dynamic layer Experimental JIT-only user-mode p-code sandbox with Linux/Windows HLE Constraint solving No native solver crate Experimental AIG/CNF, CDCL SAT, bit-vector, array, and path-feasibility foundations Change governance Pass-gate architecture ADR-backed anti-overfit, ISA-neutral ownership, validation-pool, and audit policies CI posture Standard lint/test/release lanes Exact parity and zero-tolerance regression gates, changed-file formatting ratchet, stronger security policy Package version Release tag v0.1.1; workspace packages still reported 0.1.0 Release tag, all workspace packages, and fission_cli --version report 0.1.2

The implementation range contains 98 non-merge commits and, before the release metadata update, changes 315 files with approximately 41,110 insertions and 2,113 deletions. Much of that growth is isolated in the new experimental emulator and solver crates rather than the core decompiler path.

docs.title: Variable identity and storage roles
docs.text:
0.1.2 makes variable recovery less dependent on a single physical register or stack storage identity. Important changes include:

  • preventing variable merge from collapsing simultaneously live general-purpose registers;
  • splitting incompatible storage roles across program phases, such as a pointer cursor, loaded byte, loop index, and scalar accumulator sharing one machine location over time;
  • tracking address contributors through redefinitions instead of losing pointer evidence at the most recent assignment;
  • preserving transitive address-local widths and exact scalar-local constraints;
  • improving RC4-style byte-state recovery and unsigned low-byte truncation;
  • recovering RC4 byte-sum indices formed through zero extension and arithmetic;
  • keeping primary return materializations when the ABI return register remains live at function exit; and
  • narrowing signed 32-bit return values recovered through join temporaries and select expressions.

These are general data-flow and type constraints. Production behavior does not branch on benchmark function names, binary identities, or fixed addresses.

docs.title: Returns, conditions, and loops
docs.text:
The structuring and materialization pipeline now handles more common optimized and unoptimized control-flow shapes:

  • clamp-like conditional returns whose comparisons previously disappeared;
  • x86-32 diamond returns joined through a shared epilogue;
  • natural-loop returns and loop-carried self-updates;
  • x86-32 conditional-move clamp forms;
  • short-circuit diamonds whose return-value arm must remain materialized;
  • tail-of-block absolute conditional-move skips;
  • return-bearing segments in if/goto fusion;
  • epilogue joins where the external condition-code seed is not a valid ownership barrier; and
  • signed INT_MIN presentation and partial-register xor plus setcc composition.

The work is deliberately shape-based. It uses CFG, dominance, liveness, definition/use, ABI, and type facts rather than recognizing named corpus rows.

docs.title: x86 recovery details
docs.text:
• x86 register-space EFLAGS bits receive stable names for Jcc recovery.
  • dead EFLAGS assignments are removed when they cannot affect observable output.
  • x86-32 stack parameters are recovered from calling-convention and stack facts.
  • logical right-shift parameters are typed as unsigned only in the logical-shift context.
  • partial-register zero extension and low-byte masks feed type recovery without forcing a whole-register identity merge.

docs.title: SLEIGH and Ghidra p-code parity
docs.text:
0.1.2 includes a focused parity slice for Fission's native SLEIGH runtime.

  • inst_next is resolved with a two-pass decode/lift strategy so RIP-relative forms with trailing immediates use the correct next-instruction address.
  • Relative p-code BRANCH targets are interpreted relative to the current p-code operation where required.
  • USEROP indices are dense and stable across compiled specifications.
  • Size-aware flag operations preserve operand-width semantics.
  • Floating-point conversion operation names and lowering are aligned with the shared p-code contract used by parity tooling.
  • Runtime decode, template walking, function lifting, and raw p-code serialization were updated together so CLI telemetry and internal execution see the same IR.

Ghidra is used as a structural reference for assembly, p-code, CFG, and function inventory diagnostics. Fission does not attempt to reproduce Ghidra's C syntax or presentation quirks, and parity metrics are not semantic-correctness scores.

docs.title: NIR and HIR presentation
docs.text:
The CLI now exposes explicit decompiler presentation layers:

fission_cli decomp program.exe --addr 0x140001000 --layer nir
fission_cli decomp program.exe --addr 0x140001000 --layer hir
  • NIR presentation keeps lower-level storage and data-flow evidence useful for decompiler diagnosis.
  • HIR presentation applies higher-level rendering intended for human inspection.
  • JSON output records the selected layer so automation does not need to infer it from formatting.
  • Printer changes include improved signed constants, return expressions, type spelling, and structured control-flow rendering.

Consumers that compare pseudocode text byte-for-byte should expect snapshot updates. Prefer JSON fields and semantic tests for stable automation.

docs.title: Experimental native sandbox and emulator
docs.text:
0.1.2 introduces fission-emulator and connects it to the CLI through the experimental sandbox command. This work is an execution foundation, not a claim of general binary compatibility.

Implemented foundations include:

  • a JIT-only p-code execution path based on Cranelift;
  • page-based memory, register-space mapping, loader integration, and execution budgets;
  • PE mapping and selected Windows x64 HLE procedures;
  • ELF mapping, selected Linux syscalls, dynamic-linker scaffolding, relocations, and selected libc HLE procedures;
  • architecture and calling-convention descriptions separated from OS behavior;
  • copy-on-write symbolic memory and taint propagation;
  • snapshots, time-travel state integration, and Semantic Replay Diff telemetry;
  • exploration techniques, a SimulationManager-style state set, path-feasibility pruning, and deterministic instruction limits; and
  • structured metrics for unimplemented operations, exits, HLE calls, and budget exhaustion.

The included ELF and PE fixtures are small smoke and diagnostic programs. Real programs can still stop on unsupported p-code, syscalls, relocations, floating point behavior, dynamic linking, or OS APIs. Sandbox output and command-line options may change in subsequent 0.x releases.

docs.title: Experimental native solver
docs.text:
The new fission-solver crate provides foundations used by symbolic execution:

  • expression AST simplification;
  • AIG construction and bit blasting;
  • CNF generation;
  • CDCL SAT solving with watched propagation, VSIDS, phase saving, assumptions, and model extraction;
  • initial bit-vector and array-theory support; and
  • incremental path queries and CEGAR-oriented memory reasoning.

This is not yet a drop-in replacement for mature SMT solvers. Theory coverage, floating-point semantics, diagnostics, and performance remain under active development. The APIs are experimental and are not covered by the stable CLI contract.

docs.title: Loader and dynamic-analysis changes
docs.text:
• ELF loading now exposes additional mapping and dynamic-linking information needed by the emulator.
  • PE/COFF loading exposes imports and image facts used by Windows HLE.
  • Function views preserve more precise boundaries for downstream static and emulation consumers.
  • The dynamic debugger can select an emulator backend while retaining the platform debugger abstraction.
  • TTD snapshots can carry additional execution state required for deterministic replay.

docs.title: Architecture and anti-overfit guardrails
docs.text:
0.1.2 adds process and module-level controls intended to keep decompiler fixes general:

  • ADR 0006 requires an owner proof, invariant proof, and validation matrix before semantic quality changes.
  • ADR 0007 prevents benchmark identifiers from entering AI review prompts and defines an unseen patch-validation pool.
  • ADR 0008 narrows fission-pcode toward an IR and semantic substrate, with explicit analysis, normalization, and structuring ownership boundaries.
  • ADR 0009 requires ISA-neutral semantic rules unless architecture-specific facts are supplied through the architecture abstraction.
  • SKILL.md packages the decompiler improvement workflow for use in other AI development environments.
  • Audit scripts scan for benchmark smells, prompt leaks, architecture-isolation violations, NIR boundary violations, regression risk, and metric gaming.
  • Gate policy is versioned in TOML rather than hidden in CI conditionals.

These controls do not prove that every recovery rule is universally correct. They make the evidence and ownership required for each rule reviewable.

docs.title: Automation, CI, and security
docs.text:
• CI now enforces zero-tolerance regression and exact-match parity lanes where the corresponding contract is designated exact.
  • Changed Rust files are checked by a formatting ratchet without requiring an unrelated repository-wide rewrite.
  • Automation quality policy is separated from report rendering and loaded from a versioned configuration file.
  • Fast-gate jobs cover lint, security, architecture policy, and targeted test surfaces before release tagging is allowed.
  • Cargo advisory policy was updated for the Cranelift/memmap dependency context; the exception is narrowly scoped and documented rather than globally ignored.
  • Release tags are still created only by the Release Tag (CI green) workflow after the selected commit has a successful Fast Gate run.

docs.title: CLI surface
docs.text:
The release CLI includes the following top-level command families:

info, list, disasm, raw-pcode, pcode-stages, nir-stats,
pcode-topology, decomp, strings, xrefs, callgraph, inventory,
resources, script, debug, sandbox, ai

sandbox and its symbolic/exploration features are experimental. Existing metadata, listing, disassembly, p-code, decompilation, resource, and automation commands remain the primary release surface.

docs.title: Compatibility and migration notes
docs.text:
• The executable name remains fission_cli.
  • Release archives continue to bundle required signatures, Ghidra-derived data, and SLEIGH resources under the existing layout.
  • fission_cli --version now reports 0.1.2; 0.1.1 archives could report the older workspace package version despite the release tag.
  • Decompiled pseudocode is expected to differ because variable identity, types, returns, and control flow have changed.
  • Tools parsing human-readable pseudocode should migrate to JSON output where a machine-readable mode exists.
  • The new NIR/HIR layer selector should be set explicitly in snapshot-based automation.
  • No compatibility guarantee is made yet for Rust APIs in fission-emulator or fission-solver.

docs.title: Validation evidence
docs.text:
The release candidate is validated through the repository release gate, which includes:

  • workspace metadata and locked dependency resolution;
  • cargo check for the release CLI and affected semantic crates;
  • the fission-pcode nextest suite;
  • automation policy tests;
  • lint, formatting, architecture-policy, and security checks; and
  • cross-platform CLI builds performed by tag-driven CD for Linux x86-64, macOS arm64, and Windows x86-64.

The CD workflow also runs fission_cli --version, packages resource data, produces SHA-256 checksums, and performs a Linux benchmark-consumer smoke check.

docs.title: Benchmark interpretation
docs.text:
The last checked 0.1.1 benchmark envelope is a diagnostic baseline, not evidence of 0.1.2 improvement. Its layered Fission-to-Ghidra parity report recorded:

  • assembly comparable-row match: 79.2% (95/120), 100% coverage;
  • p-code opcode-sequence agreement: 96.7%;
  • p-code loose and strict full-row agreement: 66.7%;
  • CFG block-start Jaccard: 98.8% and edge Jaccard: 96.8%, while exact CFG set agreement remained 7.8%; and
  • function-inventory presence recall: 94.6%, with exact set agreement at 50.0%.

Those values identify the remaining work; they are not a multi-decompiler quality ranking and do not establish semantic equivalence. The benchmark repository is pinned to v0.1.2 after release assets are published, then rerun so 0.1.2 receives its own provenance-linked measurements.

docs.title: Post-release verification
docs.text:
After the release assets were published, the Fission benchmark default was pinned to v0.1.2 and the push smoke workflow was rerun against the release-backed container. The workflow verified the container version before measurement and reported FISSION_RUN_VALID=true.

The strict Fission-to-Ghidra parity smoke covered 20 selected subjects and reported:

  • assembly parity: 20/20 matched, 100% coverage;
  • p-code parity: 20/20 matched, with opcode, loose-full, and strict-full rates all at 100%;
  • CFG parity: 20/20 matched, 100% coverage;
  • function discovery: 4/4 inventory rows matched; and
  • IR invariant diagnostics: 20/20 matched.

The conservative reliability aggregate contained 84 usable telemetry rows with 100% coverage, no fetch errors, and a 100% headline match rate. The accompanying multi-decompiler smoke contained 90 rows across nine tools and passed its run validity and dashboard-data gates.

This was a bounded push smoke (benchmark_limit=10, one compiler variant, and parity_limit=20), not the full official corpus. It is release-consumer and regression evidence only; it must not be substituted for a full semantic, holdout, or cross-architecture evaluation.

Workflow evidence:

https://github.com/sjkim1127/fission-benchmark/actions/runs/29253009983

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Decompiler semantic correctness remains incomplete, particularly on complex aliasing, optimized data flow, indirect control flow, and broad multi-ISA corpora.
  • Exact CFG and function-inventory parity with Ghidra is not yet reached.
  • Raw p-code full equality can differ even when opcode sequences agree because varnodes, address spaces, and function-discovery policies remain stricter.
  • The emulator supports selected user-mode paths and HLE procedures, not a full operating system or complete ISA/OS matrix.
  • The solver has incomplete theory coverage and is intended for controlled path queries rather than unrestricted SMT workloads.
  • Readability proxy metrics remain unvalidated until the planned human comprehension study; they are not a release-quality score.
  • Benchmark semantic results remain separate from structural parity and source similarity.

docs.title: Release assets
docs.text:
Tag-driven CD publishes:

  • fission-linux-x64.tar.gz
  • fission-linux-x64.sha256
  • fission-macos-arm64.tar.gz
  • fission-macos-arm64.sha256
  • fission-windows-x64.zip
  • fission-windows-x64.sha256
  • fission_cli-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
  • fission_cli-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.sha256

Verify each download with its corresponding .sha256 asset before use.

docs.title: Full comparison
docs.text:
See the complete commit and file comparison:

https://github.com/sjkim1127/Fission/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2

docs.title: Fission v0.1.3
docs.text:
Fission 0.1.3 is a decompiler-quality and release-operations patch. Compared with 0.1.2, it improves register and stack function-pointer call recovery on x86/x64 and x86-32, tightens return and condition handling around CallInd and BranchInd, lands the typed program-metadata substrate, and hardens the conservative release-tag gates used to publish SemVer assets.

This remains a pre-1.0 patch: the documented CLI is the primary interface, and pseudocode can continue to improve as recovery rules tighten.

docs.title: Release summary
docs.text: Area v0.1.2 v0.1.3 Function-pointer calls Register CallInd often opaque / incomplete Opaque cast-callable form for register CallInd; ABI arg recovery Tail calls BranchInd fp tails mostly ARM-oriented x86/x64 jmp reg tail-call recovery with staged arg peel x86-32 cdecl Stack arg / return binding gaps ESP-staged args, frame restage, EAX call-result binding, stack-param freeze Return join Pre-call EAX arg could win over call result Diamond join prefers live call-result binding Null-check conditions test reg after redef could surface wrong param Flag-test peel stops at tested register; freeze Copy source at def site Program metadata Loader facts only Typed fission-analysis-db substrate wired into decompiler preflight CI / release Standard lanes L0 Fast + L1 Heavy on main, L2 Release E2E before tag, L3 CD with utils bundle Package version 0.1.2 0.1.3 (all workspace packages + fission_cli --version)

docs.title: CallInd and BranchInd (function pointers)
docs.text:
• Lower register-held CallInd as cast function-pointer calls rather than bare symbols or undeclared targets.
  • Prefer call-result bindings over pre-call argument writes to EAX/RAX at diamond joins (null → 0, non-null → (*(fp))(…)).
  • Recover x86/x64 BranchInd through a register-held function pointer as a tail call with ABI argument recovery from the BranchInd block and a single predecessor when needed.
  • When prefer_source recovers tail-call args, peel same-block Copy/ZExt so staged values (mov ecx, edx; zext rcx) surface as the source param, not the destination ABI slot.

docs.title: x86-32 cdecl CallInd / BranchInd
docs.text:
• Include X86_32 in call-result register recovery so CallInd materializes eax = (*(fp))(…) and returns bind that result.
  • Recover cdecl arguments staged as Stores to [esp+k] (k ≥ 0) before CallInd, not only push-below-ESP forms; suppress those stores as call-arg setup (avoids undeclared local_0 from staging).
  • Recover frame restage Stores to [ebp+8+4*i] before BranchInd/CallInd for O2-style tail calls; suppress param reassignment materialization.
  • Freeze staged-store RHS to the incoming stack-param surface reached by the Copy/Load chain at the staging site so later EAX reloads of the call target do not rewrite earlier args (param_2 stays param_2).

docs.title: Conditions and related recovery
docs.text:
• For x86 branch predicates from test reg, peel unique temps to the tested register but do not continue through register-to-register Copy (avoids mov rax, rcx; mov ecx, edx; test rax becoming a null-check on param_2).
  • Lower tested values for branch predicates with Copy sources frozen at the defining site.
  • Additional loop-carried, cmov, setnz/neg, and flag-recovery CFG fixes landed between 0.1.2 and 0.1.3 and are covered by crate nextest.

docs.title: Typed program metadata
docs.text: • Add and wire fission-analysis-db as the immutable typed program-metadata snapshot consumed by decompilation preflight (ADR-aligned ownership).

docs.title: CI / CD and packaging
docs.text:
• Conservative release gates: no auto-tag on every main push; Release Tag (CI green) requires L0 Fast (push) + L1 Heavy on the SHA, then L2 Release E2E, then annotated tag → L3 CD.
  • Heavy main-push runs the release-critical crate set (extended suite on nightly/dispatch only).
  • CD publishes platform CLI archives (each with verified utils/) and a standalone fission-utils.tar.gz on the SemVer release.

docs.title: Validation notes (local quality loop)
docs.text:
Local fission-benchmark docker against a local Fission build (not official Pages / results/latest.json) measured semantic recovery for advanced_patterns / apply_binop:

Compiler variant Status after 0.1.3 work gcc -O0 6/6 gcc -O2 6/6 gcc-m32 -O0 6/6 gcc-m32 -O2 6/6

Host semantic harness for m32 uint formals widens pointer-carrying formals for host recompilation (arm64 cannot use -m32); that adaptation lives in the fission-benchmark runner and does not change decompiler surface types.

docs.title: Package and tooling versions
docs.text:
• Release tag: v0.1.3
  • All workspace crate versions: 0.1.3
  • fission_cli --version reports 0.1.3

docs.title: Upgrade notes
docs.text:
• No intentional CLI contract break relative to 0.1.2.
  • Pseudocode for function-pointer and x86-32 stack-call patterns may change shape (more cast-callable calls, fewer opaque branchind/local_0 artifacts).
  • Operators installing from GitHub Release should pin matching fission-utils.tar.gz from the same SemVer tag when using the standalone resource bundle.

docs.title: Fission v0.1.4
docs.text:
Fission 0.1.4 is a readability and architecture release. Compared with 0.1.3, it ships a contracted dual-layer HIR presentation surface (ADR 0011), extracts the post-lift midend into dedicated owner crates (ADR 0012 Phase D), and hardens CI Fast Gate so release assets stay reproducible under layered L0–L3 gates.

This remains a pre-1.0 patch: the documented CLI is the primary interface. NIR remains the semantic oracle; HIR is a clone-only readability polish layer that must not retarget official rankings.

Tagged commit: 8b5d7429 Release: https://github.com/sjkim1127/Fission/releases/tag/v0.1.4

docs.title: Release summary
docs.text: Area v0.1.3 v0.1.4 Pseudocode surfaces Dual print emerging; HIR polish ad hoc Dual-layer contract: NIR oracle + HIR presentation (ADR 0011) HIR control sugar Limited goto/alias polish Pure if/else → select, empty-then invert, const-left commute, !(eq/ne) peel HIR safety No runtime structural firewall Post-pass invariants + pre-polish restore; whole-function dead-elim Midend layout Mostly under fission-pcode (nir/midend) fission-midend-core / normalize / structuring own substrate + free-fns Structuring API PreviewBuilder-heavy methods StructuringHost + free-fn owners; pcode thin residual only Orchestration Re-exports through pcode midend paths orchestrate / pass/structuring call fission_midend_* free-fns directly Fast Gate CI Multi-OS PR cost, sccache tag issues Path-filter lanes, sccache v0.10.0 tag, analysis-db on Linux tests Package version 0.1.3 0.1.4 (all workspace packages + lockfile)

docs.title: HIR presentation (ADR 0011)
docs.text: Owner: crates/fission-pcode/src/render/presentation/ Contract: clone-only polish, same call/load evaluation count/order, no oracle swap.

docs.title: New / extended presentation passes
docs.text:
• Pure diamond folds
  • if (c) { x = a; } else { x = b; } → x = c ? a : b (presentation-pure only)
  • if (c) return a; else return b; → return c ? a : b
  • if (c) return a; return b; → ternary return
  • x = seed; if (c) { x = a; } → x = c ? a : seed (cond/then may substitute seed for x)
  • Empty-arm cleanup
  • Strip empty else {}
  • if (c) {} else { body } → if (!c) { body }
  • Condition canonicalize
  • Const-left comparisons → var-left with flipped op (0 < x → x > 0)
  • !(x == 0) / !(x != 0) peel; Bool-only double-not peel
  • Existing polish retained: param-home alias fold, self-update fold, goto shared return expand, if/while recovery from gotos, while (1) { if (!c) break; } → while (c), single-use pure inline, cast peel, dead pure flag/temp drop

docs.title: Structural presentation firewall
docs.text:
After the fixed-point polish, apply_hir_presentation checks structural invariants against the pre-polish tree (presentation/invariants.rs):

Code Meaning use_without_def New non-param uses without any assign (over-DCE) call_count_increased Real call sites increased (multi-eval risk) load_count_increased Load sites increased empty_if_shell Empty if shells increased vs pre-polish

On violation, presentation restores the pre-polish tree so broken HIR never ships. Dead pure-assign elimination uses whole-function use counts (nested subtree-local maps incorrectly dropped branch defs still live after the if).

docs.title: Validation (HIR)
docs.text:
• Focused nextest under presentation / layered
  • scripts/check/owner_boundaries.sh (render must not import normalize/structuring)
  • External fission-benchmark local docker loop for dual-layer readability proxies (not official Pages ranking)

docs.title: Owner crates
docs.text: Crate Role fission-midend-core Shared IR (Hir*), action pipeline, wave stats, labels, VSA fission-midend-normalize Full normalize pipeline (arith, idioms, memory, recovery, types) fission-midend-structuring CFG facts, SESE, collapse rules, loops/switch/conditionals free-fns, StructuringHost fission-pcode p-code IR/optimizer, builder/orchestrate, PreviewBuilder host residual, dual-layer render, public re-exports

docs.title: Structuring free-function extraction (Phase D)
docs.text:
• Introduce StructuringHost; production host is PreviewBuilder (host_impl.rs).
  • Free-fn owners for conditionals, loops, switch, linear body/recovery/multiblock, guarded-tail promote/canonicalize/suffix pure, SESE discovery/driver, orphan repair, collapse-rule dispatch.
  • pass/structuring and orchestrate call midend free-fns directly (structure_cfg_via_sese, build_sese_region_body, try_repair_orphan_gotos, build_linear_multiblock_body, normalize_hir_function).
  • Remove dead PreviewBuilder inherent thin wraps (loops/conditionals/switch/orphan/ linear test path). Linear tests call free-fns.
  • Residual intentionally left in pcode: p-code opcode tables, binary/type_context inventory adapters, some guarded-tail telemetry mark hooks.

docs.title: Dual-layer print ownership
docs.text:
• Dual-layer printer lives at crate-root render/ (not under midend normalize).
  • ADR 0013: midend diagnostic format_expr_key is not the dual-layer print surface.

docs.title: Fast Gate (L0)
docs.text:
• Path-filter lanes: docs/scripts vs rust; reduce PR multi-OS cost; multi-OS retained on main.
  • sccache via GHA backend; release tag pin fixed to v0.10.0 (bare 0.10.0 404’d).
  • Linux tests include fission-analysis-db (typed metadata; ADR 0010).
  • Clippy -D warnings: publish VSA CircleRange so public solve/range_of/eval_expr surfaces stay valid.

docs.title: Release gates (unchanged policy, exercised for this tag)
docs.text:
Layer Workflow v0.1.4 status on 8b5d7429 L0 ci.yml Fast Gate success L1 ci-heavy.yml success L2 release-e2e.yml (via Release Tag) success Tag release-tag.yml → annotated v0.1.4 success L3 cd.yml multi-platform assets success

Note: tags created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN do not re-trigger CD; this release re-pushed the annotated tag with operator credentials so L3 ran.

docs.title: Crate / unit
docs.text:
• cargo nextest run -p fission-pcode (full suite green at merge)
  • cargo check -p fission-decompiler (orchestration surface stable)
  • Owner boundary scan green

docs.title: External benchmark (quality loop, not Pages promotion)
docs.text:
Local fission-benchmark against local Fission builds measured HIR vs NIR readability proxies on smoke / core_c_pe slices (goto/homes/line reductions on HIR). Official Pages rankings remain release-baked only.

Post-release: pin fission-benchmark CI/images to v0.1.4 and run the fission-release repository_dispatch so official/smoke pipelines consume the new GitHub Release bake.

docs.title: Package and tooling versions
docs.text:
• Release tag: v0.1.4
  • All workspace crate versions: 0.1.4
  • fission_cli --version reports 0.1.4
  • Assets (each CLI archive includes verified utils/):
  • fission-linux-x64.tar.gz
  • fission-macos-arm64.tar.gz
  • fission-windows-x64.zip
  • fission-utils.tar.gz (+ inventory / sha256)

docs.title: Upgrade notes
docs.text:
• No intentional CLI contract break relative to 0.1.3.
  • HIR text may change substantially (more ternaries, fewer empty else arms, fewer param homes, fewer residual gotos). NIR remains the semantic oracle for automation/benchmark case scores.
  • Consumers of internal Rust APIs should prefer:
  • fission_pcode::midend (stable re-exports), or
  • fission_midend_core / fission_midend_normalize / fission_midend_structuring for owner-specific work.
  • Install operators should pin matching fission-utils.tar.gz from the same SemVer tag when using the standalone resource bundle.
  • fission-benchmark operators: set FISSION_VERSION=v0.1.4 (release source only for official/Pages).

docs.title: Commit range
docs.text:
v0.1.3 … v0.1.4 (8b5d7429), ~39 commits spanning HIR presentation, midend crate extraction, and CI gate hardening. Representative themes:

  • feat(hir) / feat(pcode) — HIR presentation passes
  • refactor(midend) / refactor(structuring) / refactor(pcode) — ADR 0012
  • ci: — Fast Gate lanes, sccache, analysis-db coverage
  • ADRs 0011, 0012, 0013 documentation

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This update focuses on project maturity and operator-surface cleanup rather than decompiler semantic changes.

The main goals of this wave were:

  • make benchmark/full_benchmark/ the canonical benchmark surface
  • move benchmark configuration ownership under benchmark/config/
  • align CI/reusable workflows with the new benchmark and artifact roots
  • stabilize the human-facing fission_cli subcommand surface
  • add a detailed CLI reference for evaluators and headless users

The initial maturity wave below did not intentionally change Rust decompiler semantics. The follow-up wrapper-contraction section added later in this changelog does change the explicit rust-sleigh decompilation path for proven wrapper families.

docs.title: 0. Wrapper Contraction And Explicit Pathological Function Relief
Scope
docs.text:
This follow-up wave introduced a canonical wrapper-contraction path for zero-size direct tail wrappers and aligned the CLI rust-sleigh execution path to use the same owner.

Primary target:

  • test_functions.exe @ 0x140002d40
  • function name: register_frame_ctor
  • prior family attribution: ZeroSizeRuntimeWrapper

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
• Added typed procedure-summary vocabulary for wrapper contraction in fission-pcode.
  • Added a narrow direct-tail-wrapper proof for p-code / first-instruction decode.
  • Added additive telemetry:
  • procedure_summary_contracted_count
  • procedure_summary_tail_wrapper_count
  • procedure_summary_import_thunk_count
  • Added an early wrapper probe in fission-decompiler-core that:
  • decodes only the first instruction
  • proves direct tail-wrapper shape
  • emits a minimal synthetic wrapper HIR
  • skips the giant normalize / structuring path entirely when the proof completes
  • Removed duplicate rust-sleigh rendering logic from the canonical CLI decomp execution path and routed it through fission-decompiler-core.

This is the important ownership cleanup in this wave:

  • before: crates/fission-cli/.../decompile_exec/run.rs had its own local rust-sleigh render path
  • after: the canonical decomp path consumes the shared fission-decompiler-core rust-sleigh path, so wrapper contraction is not bypassed

docs.title: Explicit pathological row improvement
docs.text:
Before this wave, the explicit pathological wrapper row was dominated by giant-function cost:

  • wall time: roughly 247s

  • structuring_duration_ms=90686
  • render_duration_ms=4
  • replacement_plan_candidate_count=39381
  • materialization_stabilized_count=33633

After this wave:

  • command:
  • target/debug/fission_cli decomp benchmark/binary/x86-64/window/small/binary/c/test_functions.exe --addr 0x140002d40 --json
  • wall time:
  • 0.91s
  • emitted code:
  • undefined register_frame_ctor() { return __gcc_register_frame(); }
  • preview build stats on the explicit row:
  • procedure_summary_contracted_count=1
  • procedure_summary_tail_wrapper_count=1
  • procedure_summary_import_thunk_count=0
  • replacement_plan_candidate_count=0
  • materialization_stabilized_count=0
  • normalize_duration_ms=0
  • structuring_duration_ms=0
  • rendered_code_len=71

Net effect:

  • explicit wrapper wall time dropped by well over 99%
  • giant normalize / materialization / structuring explosion is bypassed for the proven wrapper family

docs.title: Benchmark contract fix discovered during validation
docs.text:
Running a single-binary same-axis benchmark exposed two benchmark-side contract bugs:

  1. manifest-less single-binary runs were still injecting the fixed putty row-fidelity watchlist
  2. dynamic row-watchlist objects were passed into tuple-only row-fidelity code paths during baseline comparison

Both were fixed in benchmark/full_benchmark/grand_finale_support/benchmark_core.py:

  • manifest-less single-binary runs now default to no fixed watchlist
  • row-fidelity targets are normalized before snapshot / baseline-gate evaluation

This was necessary to make the same-axis benchmark result interpretable for test_functions.exe.

docs.title: Duplicate-logic audit outcome
docs.text:
Duplicate logic reduced in this wave:

  • wrapper summary classification is shared through procedure_summary.rs
  • the canonical CLI rust-sleigh decompile path now routes through fission-decompiler-core
  • the previous local rust-sleigh render implementation in decompile_exec/run.rs no longer bypasses wrapper contraction

Next owner after this wave:

  • keep wrapper families under the shared procedure-summary owner
  • move next to broader interprocedural wrapper/adaptor summaries only if evidence shows additional zero-cost wrappers beyond the direct-tail family
  • do not reopen the previous giant explicit wrapper path unless a non-wrapper pathological family remains after summary contraction

docs.title: 0.1 Structuring Admission Canonicalization For Windows Small C Samples
Scope
docs.text:
This follow-up wave targeted the first unresolved sample-user-code owner after wrapper contraction:

  • canonical surface:
  • benchmark/full_benchmark/
  • benchmark/binary/x86-64/window/small/binary/c
  • primary owner:
  • crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/structuring/driver.rs
  • representative row:
  • test_functions.exe @ 0x140001470
  • function name: fibonacci

The goal in this wave was not to broadly relax structuring policy. The goal was to:

  • remove the old architecture-specific blanket force-linear admission
  • make force-linear fallback reasons explicit and typed
  • expose the fallback owner in NirBuildStats
  • validate the resulting owner split on the Windows small C corpus without regressing row fidelity

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
The structuring driver now uses one canonical admission helper instead of scattered fallback shortcuts:

  • added:
  • StructuringAdmissionReason
  • StructuringAdmissionInput
  • decide_structuring_admission(...)
  • admission families are now:
  • GraphCollapse
  • ExplicitForceLinear
  • IrreducibleBudget
  • ExtremeBudget
  • removed the old x64-specific blanket admission that forced linear structuring for medium-sized 64-bit CFGs based only on block/op count

Additive telemetry was also added to NirBuildStats:

  • structuring_force_linear_explicit_count
  • structuring_force_linear_irreducible_budget_count
  • structuring_force_linear_extreme_budget_count

This keeps the policy owner inside nir/structuring/ and avoids re-encoding fallback attribution in benchmark/reporting layers.

docs.title: Negative result that was intentionally not shipped
docs.text:
During validation, a broader irreducible admission trial was tested locally so fibonacci could bypass the linear fallback.

That trial was rejected because it caused:

  • row_fidelity_gate failed for 0x140001470
  • generic_local_name_sum: 311 -> 313 on test_functions.exe

  • replacement_plan_candidate_count: 1258 -> 10978

The final shipped state stays fail-closed for that family and keeps only:

  • typed fallback attribution
  • additive telemetry

docs.title: Duplicate-logic audit outcome
docs.text:
Duplicate logic was reduced in this wave:

  • force-linear admission now has one canonical owner in structuring/driver.rs
  • the old medium-CFG x64 shortcut is no longer duplicated as an implicit architecture policy
  • benchmark/reporting now consume typed telemetry instead of reconstructing why a row fell back

Next owner after this wave:

  • Proof-Carrying Region Structuring
  • specifically:

  • guarded-tail rejection narrowing
  • region legality / switch readiness subtype narrowing
  • not:

docs.title: 0.2 BlockGraph / FlowBlock Proof Substrate For Structuring
Scope
docs.text:
This follow-up wave added a clean-room proof substrate for Ghidra-style BlockGraph / FlowBlock reasoning inside the Fission structuring owner.

Primary owner:

  • crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/structuring/

Reference model:

  • Ghidra FlowBlock, BlockGraph, and ActionStructureTransform concepts were used as architecture guidance only.
  • No Ghidra source code, runtime dependency, generated migration, or binding was introduced.

This wave is diagnostic/refactor-only from a decompiler behavior perspective:

  • wave_type: diagnostic/refactor-only
  • behavior_changed: no
  • release_path_changed: no
  • env_gate: none

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
The structuring owner now has typed BlockGraph proof vocabulary:

  • BlockGraphRegionKind
  • Sequence
  • If
  • IfElse
  • Loop
  • Switch
  • GuardedTail
  • Irreducible
  • BlockGraphLegalityReason
  • Complete
  • MissingFollow
  • MissingPostdom
  • SideEntry
  • SideExit
  • MustEmitLabelConflict
  • AliasInterleave
  • EmitReadyIncomplete
  • IrreducibleScc
  • Budget
  • BlockGraphRegionProof
  • entry
  • members
  • exits
  • follow
  • immediate postdominator
  • SCC id
  • legality reason
  • emit-ready flag

The guarded-tail path now records BlockGraph proof evidence through an adapter, but acceptance policy was intentionally kept unchanged. Incomplete proof remains fail-closed, and the printer still does not perform semantic repair.

Additive NirBuildStats telemetry:

  • blockgraph_region_candidate_count
  • blockgraph_region_complete_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_missing_follow_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_must_emit_label_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_emit_ready_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_irreducible_count

Benchmark/reporting now surfaces these metrics in:

  • single-binary verbose JSON/Markdown
  • corpus verbose JSON/Markdown
  • compact summary JSON
  • console summaries
  • Python contract tests

The compact summary also now carries targeted structuring rows, including:

  • test_functions.exe:fibonacci @ 0x140001470
  • math_operations.exe:fibonacci_memo
  • control-heavy degraded rows when available

cargo test -p fission-pcode -- --test-threads=1
result: 639 passed, 25 failed

The residual failure family is still guarded-tail / suffix-window structuring. This wave did not resolve the 25 -> 0 target; it made the next owner concrete.

docs.title: Duplicate-logic audit outcome
docs.text:
Duplicate semantic repair was not added.

  • builder remains limited to producing control evidence
  • structuring owns region legality
  • printer remains a renderer and label/goto cleanup layer only
  • benchmark/reporting only projects telemetry

Next owner after this wave:

  • reduce blockgraph_region_rejected_must_emit_label_count=426
  • migrate guarded-tail / suffix-window legality into the BlockGraph proof path until the current 25 residual tests are fixed
  • only after that, consider a narrow behavior-changing acceptance rule for fibonacci

docs.title: Canonical benchmark entrypoint
docs.text:
The canonical benchmark entrypoint is now documented and treated as:

python3 benchmark/full_benchmark/full_decomp_benchmark.py ...

This replaces older documentation and workflow references that still pointed at older benchmark script roots.

docs.title: Canonical benchmark roots
docs.text:
Benchmark ownership is now documented around these roots:

  • benchmark/full_benchmark/
  • benchmark/config/benchmark_corpus/
  • benchmark/artifacts/full_benchmark/
  • benchmark/artifacts/automation/

docs.title: Documentation updates
docs.text:
The benchmark/operator documentation was rewritten to reflect the current source of truth:

  • benchmark/full_benchmark/README.md
  • benchmark/BENCHMARK_GUIDE.md
  • benchmark/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md
  • README.md

These docs now consistently describe:

  • Windows-only corpus focus for the current wave
  • advisory-first corpus benchmark semantics
  • compact summary JSON as the preferred first-pass machine-readable artifact
  • putty as a primary canary, but not the only benchmark narrative

docs.title: Legacy path cleanup in docs
docs.text:
The documentation cleanup removed or replaced stale references such as:

  • artifacts/batch_benchmark_scripts/...
  • config/benchmark_corpus/...
  • older benchmark result/history ownership language

docs.title: Config ownership moved to benchmark/config/
docs.text:
Benchmark configuration ownership is now explicitly centered under:

  • benchmark/config/benchmark_corpus/*.json
  • benchmark/config/automation/sentinel_sets.toml

This makes benchmark configuration easier to reason about and separates it from older repo-root conventions.

docs.title: Corpus manifest validation
docs.text:
The Python benchmark contract tests were extended so checked-in manifests must satisfy the current suite contract.

The updated validation covers:

  • required top-level metadata
  • valid suite_tier
  • valid gate_mode
  • dynamic_watchlist_limit
  • Windows sample path scope
  • x86/x64 derivation viability
  • default artifact naming contract

Primary test file:

  • benchmark/full_benchmark/grand_finale_support/test_corpus_benchmark.py

docs.title: Workflow path updates
docs.text:
Reusable workflows were updated to match the new benchmark/config/artifact ownership model.

Updated areas include:

  • benchmark workflow rooting
  • corpus validation workflow rooting
  • automation artifact upload path
  • benchmark job summary fields

Files updated:

  • .github/workflows/_reusable/benchmark.yml
  • .github/workflows/_reusable/corpus-validation.yml
  • .github/workflows/_reusable/nir-check.yml
  • .github/CI_CD_GUIDE.md

docs.title: CI behavior intent
docs.text:
The workflow model now more clearly separates:

  • fast CI responsibilities
  • heavy validation responsibilities
  • advisory benchmark reporting
  • promotion eligibility reporting

Benchmark job summaries now explicitly surface:

  • benchmark status
  • gate mode
  • release promotion eligibility
  • x86/x64 summary visibility
  • promotion blockers when available

docs.title: 4. Benchmark Helper Script Cleanup
docs.text:
The operator/helper scripts were updated to stop referencing stale benchmark roots or stale CLI entry shapes.

Files updated:

  • benchmark/full_benchmark/validate_limit_regression.py
  • benchmark/full_benchmark/find_timeout_culprit.py

These updates primarily:

  • point to the canonical benchmark entrypoint
  • use the canonical CLI subcommand surface
  • reduce confusion during local repro and debugging loops

docs.title: Canonical subcommands
docs.text:
The CLI now consistently presents the explicit subcommand model as the canonical public surface:

  • info
  • list
  • disasm
  • decomp
  • strings
  • inventory

The goal is to keep human-facing one-shot usage separate from operator-grade inventory and batch emitters.

docs.title: Legacy flat invocation policy
docs.text:
Legacy flat invocations still exist as deprecated compatibility shims, but the messaging now makes the intended transition path explicit:

  • legacy flat syntax remains functional
  • it emits deprecation warnings
  • it normalizes into the canonical subcommand execution path

This preserves compatibility without preserving two user-facing command models.

docs.title: Help/usage polish
docs.text:
The CLI help surface was improved with:

  • clearer top-level positioning
  • explicit human-facing vs operator-facing distinction
  • subcommand-specific long descriptions
  • copy-pasteable examples per subcommand
  • updated legacy compatibility wording

Files updated:

  • crates/fission-cli/src/cli/oneshot/mod.rs

The new guide documents:

This document is intended for external teams evaluating Fission from the CLI in a headless workflow.

It explicitly covers:

  • checked-in sample binaries

  • decomp --addr --json

The purpose is to reduce evaluator uncertainty and make the CLI/output contract visible before running the tool.

docs.title: README / CLI routing updates
docs.text:
The public-facing docs were tightened so evaluators land on the right entrypoint faster.

Files updated:

  • README.md

  • keep docs/CLI.md as the detailed command reference
  • position the CLI as the primary documented product surface
  • separate manual CLI evaluation from benchmark workflows

docs.title: 9. Speed Bottleneck Wave 1: Batch Target Filtering
Goal
docs.text:
This wave targeted whole-binary CLI/operator throughput rather than NIR/core pass internals.

The focus was to stop sending obvious non-user functions through the full batch decompilation path by default.

docs.title: New default batch-selection policy
docs.text:
Whole-binary batch selection now filters the following by default:

  • imported functions (is_import == true)
  • zero-size runtime wrapper:
  • register_frame_ctor

This behavior applies to multi-function/operator surfaces such as:

  • fission_cli decomp <binary> --all
  • fission_cli inventory function-facts ...
  • shared batch/inventory selector paths used by preview-candidate workflows

It does not change exact-address behavior for:

  • --addr
  • --addresses-file
  • list
  • info

docs.title: New compatibility flag
docs.text:
A new public CLI flag was added:

  • --include-nonuser-functions

This restores the old whole-function-set behavior for batch/operator workflows when compatibility or forensics coverage is intentionally desired.

docs.title: Shared selector/accounting
docs.text:
The batch target policy is now centralized in the shared function-selection layer instead of being duplicated per command.

This also introduced stable selection accounting fields such as:

  • functions_discovered_total
  • functions_selected_total
  • functions_excluded_import_count
  • functions_excluded_runtime_wrapper_count
  • include_nonuser_functions

These counts now surface in:

  • decomp --all --json --benchmark metadata
  • inventory summary JSON
  • preview-candidate batch/inventory summary shapes where the selector contract is reused

docs.title: Validation result
docs.text:
The speed gain came from target-set reduction, not from any pass-level optimization.

Measured whole-binary comparisons on the Windows x86-64 small C corpus showed:

  • test_functions.exe
  • 258.55s -> 0.36s
  • selected 78 / 118
  • excluded imports 39
  • excluded runtime wrappers 1
  • bitops_and_control_flow.exe
  • 191.21s -> 0.25s
  • selected 80 / 120
  • excluded imports 39
  • excluded runtime wrappers 1
  • function_pointers_and_strings.exe
  • 245.04s -> 0.31s
  • selected 91 / 134
  • excluded imports 42
  • excluded runtime wrappers 1

These runs remained crash-free.

docs.title: Important boundary
docs.text:
This wave did not tune:

  • wide_dead_assignment
  • sccp
  • jump_resolver
  • break_continue_recovery

It is a throughput admission/filtering change only, not a semantic optimization wave.

docs.title: 10. Speed Bottleneck Wave 2: wide_dead_assignment Rerun Admission Trial
Goal
docs.text:
This wave targeted the remaining normalize-time bottleneck after batch filtering by narrowing only the rerun portion of wide_dead_assignment.

The first defuse_dead_assignment_pass remains mandatory. Only reruns 2..6 are now gated.

docs.title: Env gate
docs.text:
This trial is protected by a new default-off env gate:

  • FISSION_ENABLE_WIDE_DEAD_ASSIGNMENT_RERUN_ADMISSION

When the gate is enabled:

  • first pass always runs
  • reruns are admitted only if:
  • count_hir_stmts(body) <= 220
  • locals.len() <= 160
  • otherwise reruns are skipped and the first-pass result is kept

docs.title: New telemetry and reporting
docs.text:
Trial-specific telemetry was added to NirBuildStats:

  • wide_dead_assignment_rerun_admitted_count
  • wide_dead_assignment_rerun_skipped_by_admission_count

Benchmark/reporting now also surfaces selected normalize pass metrics for:

  • wide_dead_assignment
  • sccp
  • jump_resolver
  • break_continue_recovery

These metrics now appear in:

  • benchmark verbose JSON/Markdown
  • compact summary JSON
  • console summary

docs.title: Validation result
docs.text:
This wave is not promotion-ready.

Observed results:

  • Windows small C corpus limit50 same-axis benchmark:
  • quality stayed neutral
  • weighted corpus similarity stayed unchanged at 37.400%
  • no new failed rows appeared
  • whole-binary filtered decomp --all --json runs:
  • test_functions.exe: 0.40s -> 0.34s
  • bitops_and_control_flow.exe: 0.24s -> 0.23s
  • function_pointers_and_strings.exe: 0.29s -> 0.29s
  • selected row counts stayed unchanged
  • explicit pathological function check:
  • register_frame_ctor @ 0x140002d40
  • 252.25s -> 2164.06s
  • wide_dead_assignment pass time itself decreased slightly, but wall time regressed severely

docs.title: Final status
docs.text:
status: default-off negative env-gated result
env_gate: FISSION_ENABLE_WIDE_DEAD_ASSIGNMENT_RERUN_ADMISSION
release_path_changed: no
release promotion: no
reason: quality stayed neutral, but targeted speed objective failed and explicit pathological function latency regressed badly

docs.title: Practical takeaway
docs.text:
This trial shows that wide_dead_assignment rerun admission alone is not a safe next promotion candidate.

The pass-level reporting added here remains useful, but the optimization result itself should stay default-off and be treated as a negative env-gated result.

docs.title: 8. What This Wave Explicitly Did Not Change
docs.text:
This wave did not intentionally change:

  • Rust decompiler semantics
  • NIR/HIR structuring logic
  • release-path decompiler behavior
  • benchmark advisory/blocking semantics
  • inventory output schemas as part of semantic work

The focus was operational maturity, documentation clarity, and evaluation-readiness.

docs.title: 9. Practical Outcome
docs.text:
After this wave:

  • benchmark ownership is easier to find
  • CI references the current benchmark/config roots
  • fission_cli presents a cleaner headless-first surface
  • evaluators have a proper CLI reference instead of only README snippets
  • legacy CLI usage is still possible, but the intended migration path is now explicit

This prepares the repo for the next phase of work, where decompilation-quality improvements can be evaluated against a more stable benchmark and operator surface.

docs.title: 11. Speed Bottleneck Wave 3: Giant Explicit Function Cost Decomposition
Goal
docs.text:
This wave stayed diagnostic-only and targeted the remaining pathological speed owner after the batch filtering work.

The observed bottleneck was no longer filtered whole-binary --all throughput. It was the explicit giant-function path, especially:

  • test_functions.exe @ 0x140002d40
  • register_frame_ctor

The objective in this wave was to separate:

  • normalize cost
  • structuring cost
  • render cost
  • pathological replacement/materialization pressure

without changing decompiler behavior.

docs.title: New canonical telemetry
docs.text:
NirBuildStats now records additional stage-level telemetry:

  • structuring_duration_ms
  • render_duration_ms
  • rendered_code_len
  • max_structuring_scc_component_size

These were added additively. Existing fields such as:

  • build_duration_ms
  • normalize_duration_ms
  • pass_metrics
  • replacement/materialization counters

retain their existing semantics.

docs.title: New benchmark/reporting readout
docs.text:
The benchmark/reporting layer now derives giant-function speed families from the raw telemetry.

New derived families:

  • ZeroSizeRuntimeWrapper
  • NormalizeHeavy
  • StructuringHeavy
  • RenderHeavy
  • ReplacementPlanExplosion
  • MixedGiantFunction
  • UnknownGiantFunction

Per-binary and corpus summaries now surface:

  • giant_function_candidates
  • giant_function_speed_family_counts
  • giant_function_speed_family_totals
  • max_rendered_code_len
  • max_structuring_scc_component_count
  • max_replacement_plan_candidate_count
  • max_materialization_stabilized_count
  • capped max_pathological_examples

This readout is available in:

  • verbose JSON/Markdown
  • compact summary JSON
  • console summary

docs.title: Live diagnostic result
docs.text:
The filtered whole-binary path remained stable and fast:

  • test_functions.exe: 78 selected rows, crash-free
  • bitops_and_control_flow.exe: 80 selected rows, crash-free
  • function_pointers_and_strings.exe: 91 selected rows, crash-free

The explicit pathological target remained crash-free and now carries full owner telemetry:

binary: test_functions.exe
addr: 0x140002d40
name: register_frame_ctor
size: 0
build_duration_ms: 247793
normalize_duration_ms: 155567
structuring_duration_ms: 90686
render_duration_ms: 4
rendered_code_len: 452822
structuring_scc_component_count: 228
max_structuring_scc_component_size: 17
replacement_plan_candidate_count: 39381
materialization_stabilized_count: 33633
giant_function_speed_family: ZeroSizeRuntimeWrapper

docs.title: Practical conclusion
docs.text:
This wave closed a key ambiguity:

  • the pathological explicit-function cost is not render-dominant in wall time
  • normalize remains the largest stage
  • structuring is also materially large
  • replacement/materialization pressure is extreme
  • the current pathological family is now narrowly attributable as:
  • ZeroSizeRuntimeWrapper

This is enough evidence to avoid guessing at the next policy wave. The next speed step should target the explicit pathological family directly, not reopen general filtered --all throughput work.

docs.title: Final status
docs.text:
wave_type: diagnostic-only
behavior_changed: no
release_path_changed: no
env_gate: none
promotion impact: none

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This wave starts the Ghidra 1:1 clean-room migration spine without changing decompiler behavior. The goal is to make Fission's Rust-native per-function path observable through Ghidra-style owner boundaries:

This is a clean-room conceptual mapping only. No Ghidra runtime dependency, code copy, or release-path behavior change was introduced.

docs.title: Ghidra BlockGraph Must-Emit-Label Ownership And Guarded-Tail Canonicalization
Summary
docs.text:
This wave moved the next Windows small C quality owner forward in two ways:

  • the residual guarded-tail / suffix-window crate failure family was fixed
  • MustEmitLabelConflict is no longer a single opaque benchmark bucket; it now projects subtype totals through the canonical structuring owner and the benchmark layer

This is a behavior-changing quality wave, but it is still not promotion-ready. The sample corpus stayed quality-neutral while a new advisory blocker appeared in the failure-family distribution.

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
Canonical owner stayed inside:

  • crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/structuring/guarded_tail/
  • crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/types.rs

Behavioral guarded-tail / suffix-window fixes:

  • canonicalized forward-alias chains can now resolve a unique forward target even when the next label sits outside the currently canonicalized slice
  • suffix-window rejection precedence now prefers proof-first reasons:
  • nested or nonlocal tail references
  • side-effectful suffix payloads
  • nonterminal goto targets
  • unresolved alias redirects
  • candidate external-entry classification now skips the anchor statement itself, which avoids counting the candidate's own branch as an external entry
  • paired nested-boundary internalization is no longer double-counted when same-guard-family nested conditional entries already internalize the same shape

Additive BlockGraph subtype telemetry was added to NirBuildStats:

  • blockgraph_region_rejected_middle_ref_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_external_ref_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_join_owner_conflict_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_nonterminal_join_count
  • blockgraph_region_rejected_follow_owner_conflict_count

Benchmark/reporting now projects the same subtype totals through:

  • benchmark_summary.json
  • benchmark_compact_summary.json
  • corpus Markdown
  • console summaries

This keeps the semantic owner in fission-pcode; benchmark code only reads and renders the counters.

docs.title: Duplicate-logic audit
docs.text:
Duplicate semantic logic was reduced in this wave:

  • suffix-window legality and alias-chain canonicalization now resolve through the shared guarded-tail owner instead of separate overlapping ad hoc checks
  • BlockGraph subtype counters are defined once in NirBuildStats and projected outward, rather than re-derived in Python
  • benchmark/reporting remains telemetry-only and does not perform semantic repair

What did not change:

  • printer/layout code still does not own structuring legality
  • representative/materialization policy was not broadened here

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:

cargo test -p fission-pcode suffix_window -- --test-threads=1

result: 22 passed

cargo check -p fission-pcode
cargo check -p fission-automation
cargo build -p fission-cli
cargo build -p fission-cli --release

Net crate-level improvement:

full-suite residual failures: 25 -> 0
primary fixed family: guarded-tail / suffix-window / structuring candidate discovery

docs.title: What improved
docs.text:
Concrete improvements in this wave:

  • guarded-tail / suffix-window canonicalization and candidate-discovery regressions are gone at the crate-test level
  • MustEmitLabelConflict is no longer one opaque bucket in benchmark artifacts
  • the sample corpus remains stable with no new failed rows

docs.title: What regressed
docs.text:
The wave is still not promotion-ready.

New advisory blocker:

failure_family_distribution canonical_alias_interleave_conflict_count: 38 -> 50

This means the current guarded-tail canonicalization tightened one owner family but shifted pressure into alias-interleave rejection elsewhere. That is a real semantic owner signal, not a benchmark artifact issue.

docs.title: Practical conclusion
docs.text:
This wave closed the old residual test gap but did not yet deliver visible sample-corpus pseudocode quality uplift.

The current state is:

  • test-family health improved materially
  • corpus similarity is neutral
  • fibonacci is still linearized

  • GuardFamilyInternalizable

  • NestedBeforeExternalOwner

The key architectural change is ownership reuse:

  • canonicalization now consumes suffix-window guard-family / paired-boundary proof helpers
  • the nested-before classification logic stays inside guarded-tail ownership
  • duplicate semantic logic was reduced instead of adding a second ad hoc path

docs.title: 3. Benchmark Measurement Readout Tightening For Target Structuring Rows
Summary
docs.text:
This follow-up wave checked whether the benchmark was failing to measure quality movement on the current Windows small C owner.

The answer is:

  • the benchmark was not blind to fibonacci
  • the target row was already being measured by row-fidelity gate
  • but the compact/Markdown artifact was too coarse to show that quickly
  • and one target-row selector path was overmatching a shared bare address across binaries

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
Two additive reporting fixes were introduced.

First, target structuring rows now carry baseline/current quality readout:

  • previous_normalized_similarity
  • current_normalized_similarity
  • normalized_similarity_delta
  • row_gate_status
  • watchlist_role
  • failure_reasons

This is now projected through:

  • corpus compact summary
  • single-binary compact summary
  • corpus Markdown
  • single-binary Markdown

Second, the target-row selector no longer overmatches a shared address globally across the corpus.

Previous bug:

  • 0x140001470 was treated as a global target address
  • this incorrectly pulled function_pointers_and_strings.exe:compare_int_descending into target_structuring_rows

Current contract:

  • name-based target rows remain supported for:
  • fibonacci
  • fibonacci_memo
  • address-based targeting is now binary-scoped for the known canary row

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:

python3 -m unittest benchmark/full_benchmark/grand_finale_support/test_corpus_benchmark.py
- 25 passed / 0 failed

python3 -m unittest benchmark/full_benchmark/grand_finale_support/test_llm_advisory.py
- 7 passed / 0 failed

Added checks:

  • target structuring rows inherit row-gate delta fields
  • shared-address rows from unrelated binaries no longer overmatch

  • the benchmark was already measuring the current canary rows correctly
  • the current semantic wave simply did not change their emitted pseudocode

docs.title: Follow-up completion: canonical quality rows + code hash + verbose summary parity
docs.text:
The initial target-row delta tightening exposed one real implementation gap:

  • build_comparison() was wired for canonical_quality_rows only partially
  • single-binary verbose summaries were written before target_structuring_rows were re-annotated from baseline row-gate
  • as a result, compact summary had the right answer, but single-binary verbose summary could still hide unchanged target rows

That follow-up is now closed.

Additional changes:

  • manifest-owned canonical_quality_rows added to:
  • benchmark/config/benchmark_corpus/windows_small_c_samples.json
  • row-fidelity snapshot now preserves:
  • canonical_quality_rows

  • target rows now project:

  • previous_code_sha256
  • code_changed
  • compact summary now emits:
  • unchanged_target_rows
  • verbose corpus + verbose single benchmark summaries now also emit:
  • Unchanged Target Rows

Summary
docs.text:
This wave implemented the next narrow clean-room migration slice under the canonical guarded-tail owner:

  • owner: crates/fission-pcode/src/nir/structuring/guarded_tail/
  • focus: MustEmitLabel gate classification
  • scope: same-guard-family nested-before refs before a guarded-tail candidate

The goal was not to broaden structuring generally. It was to stop collapsing all pre-candidate nested refs into the same external-reference bucket.

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
The MustEmitLabel gate now classifies outside refs with more structure:

  • top-level-before
  • nested-before
  • top-level-after
  • nested-after

Narrow admission added:

  • same-guard-family nested-before refs are internalized for join-owner accounting
  • unrelated nested-before refs stay fail-closed and are now counted as owner_conflict

Concrete implementation points:

  • promotion.rs
  • replaced the old raw outside_refs threshold logic with typed site accounting
  • promotion_graph.rs
  • added internalized_guard_family_nested_before_refs_for_join_owner(...)
  • suffix_window.rs
  • reused exprs_share_guard_family(...) across the guarded-tail owner
  • structuring_guarded_tail.rs
  • updated the discovery regression to expect owner_conflict for the unresolved nested-before family

docs.title: 5. CLI Process CPU / Worker Telemetry For Parallel Decompilation
Summary
docs.text:
This wave added first-class process CPU and worker readout to the CLI benchmark JSON path so hot-machine / fan-noise reports can be separated from actual Fission CPU behavior.

The immediate investigation found:

  • no active fission_cli, benchmark, Ghidra Java, cargo, or rustc process during the heat report
  • the dominant CPU process was VS Code Code Helper (Plugin) PID 19578
  • that process was tied to the Continue extension and .continue/index/index.sqlite-shm

So the current heat event was not caused by a running Fission decompilation job.

docs.title: What changed
docs.text:
fission_cli decomp ... --json --benchmark now emits additive _meta fields:

  • worker_count
  • worker_fanout_enabled
  • available_parallelism
  • worker_env_requested
  • decomp_stack_mb
  • cpu_user_sec
  • cpu_system_sec
  • cpu_total_sec
  • cpu_utilization_pct
  • effective_parallelism

The CPU values are process-local and come from getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF) on Unix/macOS. They are intentionally independent from the benchmark wrapper sampler, which can miss short Fission runs.

Benchmark summaries now project those values into:

  • verbose single-binary summary
  • verbose corpus summary
  • compact single-binary summary
  • compact corpus summary
  • per-binary compact rows

docs.title: Reading of the result
docs.text:
What improved:

  • Fission now reports its own process CPU consumption and parallelism directly in benchmark JSON.
  • The compact AI-facing artifact can distinguish wall-clock speed from total CPU burn.
  • Worker fan-out can now be evaluated as a throughput/CPU-efficiency tradeoff instead of guessed from wall time.

What did not change:

  • no decompiler semantic behavior changed
  • no worker scheduling policy changed
  • no quality metric changed

Practical interpretation:

  • current small C corpus runs are not showing an infinite-loop / runaway CPU signature
  • the workload has limited parallel efficiency at high worker counts
  • workers=4 gave the best wall time in this probe
  • workers=8 increased total CPU seconds and effective parallelism but did not improve wall time

practical_result: CPU usage is now measurable per Fission run; current heat report points outside Fission
next owner: adaptive worker policy and per-function progress guards

This wave added a default-off MIR BlockGraph behavior trial for the Ghidra FlowBlock / BlockGraph clean-room migration path.
